<par>Khaling has <latinform>-e</latinform> where other Kiranti languages have <latinform>-u</latinform> in at least two other roots: COME/BRING DOWN Hayu <latinform>ju(t)-</latinform>, Bahing <latinform>ju(t)-</latinform>, Kulung <latinform>yuw-</latinform>, <latinform>yutt-</latinform>; but Khaling <latinform>ye(n)-</latinform>. STEAL Hayu <latinform>khu(t)-</latinform>, Bahing <latinform>ku(s)-</latinform>, Thulung <latinform>khu-</latinform>, Kulung <latinform>kuss-</latinform>; but Khaling <latinform>khe-</latinform>. See Michailovsky 1991, <emph>Proto-Kiranti</emph>, pp. 15, 34.</par>
